content: Born in Kolkata, Paes lives in Goa, where his father Vece Paes comes from. While Leander's father was part of the Munich Olympics bronzewinning hockey team in 1972, his mother, Jennifer Paes, captained India's women's basketball team.

title: Why US Targeted Iran's Isfahan With 900-kg Bunker Buster Bombs
content: Isfahan is a strategic hub that houses Iran's military bases and defence industry. The central city is also home to one of three uranium enrichment sites bombed by the US in the 12-day conflict between Iran and Israel in June.

content: The H-FAST team also uncovered units producing ginger-garlic paste mixed with acetic acid, synthetic colours, gum powder and excess salt. In one raid alone, nearly 1,900 kg of unsafe paste was seized.

content: According to Dnyaneshwar Kokate's mother, his lover's family called him to meet them one day and brutally beat him up and humiliated him.

content: The PCI has advised print media to follow the Guidelines on Election Reporting and Norms of Journalistic Conduct-2022

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Election officials plan ‘halwa campaign’ to welcome first-time voters
content: Two hundred first-time voters in each district will be presented with complimentary halwa as part of the ‘Vote sweetened Kerala

title: Kerala Assembly Elections 2026: Tamil Nadu-based political parties are nearly invisible this Assembly elections
content: Although three T.N.-based parties had actively campaigned in the Tamil-populated taluks of Devikulam and Peerumade in the local body elections, they couldn’t win a single seat

title: Bengaluru techie jumps to death from 17th floor after finding partner dead in locked room
content: A techie couple from Telangana died tragically within minutes of each other in Bengaluru's Kothanur area. The husband was found hanging in a locked room, and his wife subsequently jumped from the 17th floor of their apartment building. Police are investigating multiple angles, including health, relationship issues, and emotional distress.

title: 'Consider me your candidate in all 294 seats': Mamata's pitch in Bengal battle
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee urged voters to support her leadership across all 294 constituencies, positioning herself as the face of the Trinamool Congress. She also alleged that the BJP is attempting to manipulate voter rolls by including individuals from other states and targeting deletions, accusing the party of acting in concert with the Election Commission.

title: Fast-track to Team India? Calls for Sooryavanshi's senior debut grow; Ashwin defers
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's IPL heroics have sparked calls for his fast-tracking into India's T20 squad, with former cricketers like Mohammad Kaif and Piyush Chawla backing his readiness. However, former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin urges caution, emphasising the need to manage expectations for the 15-year-old prodigy, despite acknowledging his immense talent.

title: 'So-called defender of Hinduism says nothing about Sabarimala': Rahul takes dig at PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi accused Prime Minister Modi of selectively raising religious issues, questioning his silence on the alleged Sabarimala gold theft. Gandhi claimed an understanding between the BJP and Kerala's ruling LDF, suggesting Modi controls Chief Minister Vijayan. He asserted the CPI(M) has become an extreme right-wing party, aligning with the BJP.

title: Story behind Palmer Luckey’s ‘firing’ from Facebook, co he sold his startup for $2 bn
content: Palmer Luckey, Oculus VR's founder, was acquired by Meta for $2 billion at age 21. He later left Meta amid controversy over political donations. Luckey then founded Anduril Industries, a defense-tech startup, and recently commented on Meta's VR layoffs, deeming them beneficial for the industry's long-term health.

title: 'Fierce falling contest between rupee and BJP's image': Akhilesh Yadav
content: Samajwadi Party leader Akhilesh Yadav criticized the BJP, drawing a parallel between the falling rupee and the party's tarnished image due to alleged corruption. Opposition leaders, including Congress, amplified attacks on the BJP, highlighting public suffering from rising prices of essential goods and unreliable utilities, exacerbated by the Iran war

title: US drops 900kg bomb on Isfahan: How 'bunker buster' dismantled Iran's defences
content: A formidable impact was felt as a bunker buster bomb detonated near Isfahan, targeting an Iranian ammunition stockpile and underscoring the intensified conflict. Iran's elaborate network of underground tunnels, once a shield, has transformed into a focal point for the American-Israeli coalition's military strategy.

title: Amid US-Iran war, India’s diesel exports see big 20% jump; petrol exports fall 33%
content: Although crude prices have surged amid the near shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz, the effect has differed across fuels. Crack spreads for diesel and jet fuel have climbed to new highs, whereas those for petrol have remained broadly stable.

title: Microsoft AI CEO reveals what will really decide AI's winners and losers by 2028
content: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman argues that the AI industry's future hinges on companies that can afford to run models at scale, not just build the smartest ones. He posits that inference compute scarcity will define winners and losers for the next few years, with high-margin products gaining a significant advantage through a data flywheel of continuous improvement and adoption.

title: 'Ramayana': Ranbir Kapoor bowls audience over with 'Rama' first glimpse
content: Ranbir Kapoor's 'Ramayana' teaser, 'Rama', premiered in Los Angeles, captivating audiences with its breathtaking scale and vision. Director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra joined Kapoor for a Q&A, sharing insights into the epic's creation. Attendees praised the film's emotional depth and Kapoor's respectful connection, eagerly anticipating its Diwali 2026 release.

title: After Rahane's 'ask CA' jibe; AUS head coach comes in support of Cameron Green
content: Cameron Green's all-round potential is under scrutiny as Australia's coach Andrew McDonald emphasizes balancing IPL and national team expectations. Despite a back niggle, Green's fitness won't hinder his consideration for all formats, with selectors eyeing him for the vacant No. 5 Test position.

title: Jadeja kisses CSK crest after RR win, emotional moment melts hearts
content: Ravindra Jadeja shared an emotional moment with Khaleel Ahmed after Rajasthan Royals' win over Chennai Super Kings, kissing the CSK logo on his jersey.  Jadeja reflected on his difficult but mature transition from CSK, expressing happiness to be back with Rajasthan, his first IPL franchise, where he won the inaugural title.

title: Starting April 1, USCIS alert says that the immigration authority will reject forms
content: USCIS will only accept the 02/27/26 edition of Form I-129 for H-1B and other visa applications starting April 1, 2026. The updated form requires employers to detail educational requirements and experience, aligning with the Department of Labor's wage level assignments for the new wage-weighted lottery system.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO Day 13: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 900 crore net milestone
content: 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its record-breaking run on day 13, surpassing the lifetime India net of 'Dhurandhar Part 1' in just 11 days. With a domestic net of Rs. 873.99 crore and a worldwide gross nearing Rs. 1,400 crore, the Ranveer Singh starrer is successfully defying the IPL 2026 season craze. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film is now chasing the Rs. 900 crore and eventually the Rs. 1000 crore India net milestone, a feat previously seen only by the Allu Arjun starrer 'Pushpa 2.'

title: Reporters protest as Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan answers select queries
content: In a heated exchange in Kollam, Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan found himself at odds with reporters following a press conference. Amid accusations of favoritism towards CPM-aligned media, Vijayan was confronted by mainstream journalists upset over his selective engagement.

title: 8 women killed, 6 injured in stampede at Bihar's Sheetalashtami temple
content: Tragedy struck Nalanda's Maghra area as a stampede at Mata Sheetalashtami Temple claimed eight lives and injured several devotees. The incident occurred during a large religious gathering, causing panic. Meanwhile, President Droupadi Murmu is set to grace Nalanda University's convocation in Rajgir, marking her first visit as President to the institution.

title: PrSM missile used to strike Iran: How much the US has spent on its new weapon
content: Operation Epic Fury, a US-led campaign against Iran, commenced with missile strikes on February 28.  A new weapon, the Precision Strike Missile (PrSM), was reportedly used in Lamerd, hitting a sports hall and school, causing significant casualties.  The missile's novel design and targeting capabilities raise questions about the Lamerd incident's intent and accuracy.

title: 'US respects Israel's right': Netanyahu govt approves death penalty for Palestinian attackers
content: Israel's parliament has approved a controversial law allowing the execution of Palestinians convicted of deadly terror attacks, alongside a crucial national budget. Prime Minister Netanyahu backed the bill, which critics argue creates unequal legal tracks. This dual move comes amid ongoing regional conflict and rising violence in the West Bank.

title: ‘Baat nahi hui’: Why Ravindra Jadeja hasn’t spoken to MS Dhoni yet
content: Ravindra Jadeja impressed on his Rajasthan Royals return, taking two crucial wickets against his former team Chennai Super Kings. He humorously revealed he hadn't spoken to MS Dhoni, who apparently keeps his phone off. Jadeja enjoyed the turning wicket, executing his plans effectively to help RR secure an emphatic victory.

title: Bank holidays in April 2026: When will banks remain closed? Check state-wise list
content: Banks across India will observe several closures in April 2026 due to national and state-specific holidays, including year-end finalization and Good Friday. Several states will also see branches shut for regional festivals and observances like Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Jayanti and Bengali New Year. Digital banking services will remain operational for routine transactions.

title: PSL ball-tampering row rekindles old doubts: Fakhar in the dock for a 2nd time
content: Fakhar Zaman faces a Level 3 ball-tampering charge in the PSL after an incident during Lahore Qalandars' match against Karachi Kings. This controversy echoes a similar episode during the T20 World Cup against the USA, where Zaman's actions also raised suspicions. Both incidents occurred under pressure, with no proven offense yet.

title: ‘Phones stolen during Bengaluru IPL match’: Fans after RCB vs SRH game
content: At least 25 spectators reported losing their mobile phones during an IPL match in Bengaluru between RCB and SRH. While police are investigating, they clarify that losses may have occurred both inside and outside the stadium. Seven minors have been identified and are being questioned as suspects.

title: Gold price prediction: What levels of yellow metal should investors watch out for?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold is trading as a risk asset and not a safe haven asset, says Praveen Singh, Head Currencies and Commodities, Mirae Asset ShareKhan.

title: 'Two-year ban rok di': How Mohsin Naqvi saved Naseem Shah's career in last-minute call
content: Pakistan cricketer Naseem Shah narrowly avoided a two-year ban for a controversial social media post, with PCB chief Mohsin Naqvi reportedly intervening to impose a PKR 20 million fine instead. The pacer had criticized Punjab Chief Minister Maryam Nawaz's presence at a PSL game.

title: Assam assembly elections: What is BJP offering in its poll manifesto
content: The BJP's Assam election manifesto, 'Sankalp Patra,' outlines 31 commitments focusing on indigenous rights, economic growth, and infrastructure. Key promises include investing Rs 5 lakh crore to make Assam an economic hub, strengthening law and order, and implementing a Uniform Civil Code. The party also pledges to protect land and heritage of indigenous communities and create jobs for youth.

title: Willing to end war without re-opening Hormuz, Trump tells aides as Iran refuses to budge
content: President Trump is reportedly willing to end the conflict without reopening the Strait of Hormuz, opting instead to weaken Iran's military capabilities and increase diplomatic pressure. This shift comes as an operation to unblock the strait was deemed likely to extend the conflict beyond his preferred timeline.

title: Not Kashmir! Pune wakes up to ‘snowfall’ scenes as hailstorm blankets Hinjewadi
content: Pune experienced an unexpected hailstorm, transforming its typical dusty summer into a surreal winter wonderland. The city, particularly Hinjewadi, was covered in ice marbles, sparking widespread awe and shared joy. This rare event offered a collective pause, washing away the grime and providing a memorable story for residents.

title: Candidates Chess: How Divya Deshmukh missed winning chance against R Vaishali
content: The FIDE Candidates 2026 saw a day of draws in Cyprus, with all games ending in a tie. Indian talents Divya Deshmukh and Vaishali Rameshbabu played a thrilling game, featuring a brilliant queen sacrifice by Vaishali. In the Open section, R Praggnanandhaa drew against Wei Yi, while Hikaru Nakamura salvaged a draw from a difficult position.

title: ‘DJ music muffled his cries’: UP woman, lover sedate husband, tie him to cot, slit throat
content: Police in Bijnor have arrested a woman, her lover, and his friend for the murder of her husband, Mohammad Mehraj. The victim was allegedly sedated and then murdered with a sharp weapon while sleeping. The wife reportedly staged being unconscious during the incident.

title: Trump shares video of massive explosions in Iran's Isfahan; bunker-busters used?
content: US President Donald Trump intensified his rhetoric on the military campaign against Iran, sharing a striking image of Isfahan ablaze following Operation Epic Fury. American forces reportedly used powerful bunker-buster bombs on a large ammunition depot in the city, which also houses facilities linked to Iran's nuclear program. This follows previous strikes targeting its nuclear infrastructure.

title: Florida airport to be renamed after Donald Trump
content: Palm Beach International Airport is officially set to become President Donald J. Trump International Airport following a Florida bill signed by Governor Ron DeSantis. Eric Trump announced the change, which takes effect July 1st, pending procedural updates. The Trump Organization filed a trademark for the name, assuring no financial benefit to the former president or his family from the rebranding.

title: Russian photographer’s ‘pink elephant’ shoot in Jaipur sparks animal safety concern
content: A Russian photographer's viral photoshoot featuring a bright pink-painted elephant in Jaipur has sparked online criticism and ethical concerns. The photographer stated organic, locally-sourced paint was used and the elephant was cleaned promptly after the shoot. The concept aimed to reflect Rajasthan's vibrant culture and the animal's significance.

title: Karnataka 1st PUC results 2026 declared; state board releases marks memo via official portal
content: The Karnataka School Examination and Assessment Board announced the Karnataka 1st PUC Result 2026 on March 31, enabling Class 11 students to access marks memos online via the official portal. Candidates can download their scorecards using required credentials and verify details. The board will open applications for re-evaluation and answer script scrutiny within a specified period. Students who fail in subjects can appear for supplementary examinations, with the schedule expected to be released soon.

title: TVK chief Vijay booked for violating election rules day after entering Tamil Nadu polls
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) chief Vijay faces a case for alleged election conduct violations. A complaint cited the use of 30 loudspeakers and a gathering of over 5,000 people causing public inconvenience and obstructing a pathway, reportedly blocking an ambulance. Police have registered a case against Vijay and party workers.

title: Bank holiday: Are banks open or closed today for Mahavir Jayanti? Check state-wise list
content: Many banks across India are closed today for Mahavir Jayanti, impacting customers in states like Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Uttar Pradesh. However, agency banks handling government transactions will remain open on March 31st to facilitate financial year-end dealings. Digital banking services are unaffected, but check with your bank for specific details.

title: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said no to 'birthday cake' before smashing CSK
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi starred in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 opener, smashing a rapid 52 off 17 balls against Chennai Super Kings.  He prioritized rest over birthday celebrations to deliver a match-winning powerplay performance, guiding his team to an emphatic eight-wicket victory.  The young sensation credited his coaches and opening partner for his confident display.

title: 'Didn't expect this': American tourist in India reflects on a journey in India that changed him
content: An American tourist shares his transformative journey in India. He found unexpected kindness amidst the chaos. This experience deeply affected him, making him feel a sense of belonging. He emphasizes that India is not just visited but felt. The memories and feelings stay with him long after his departure.

title: 'Agastya, are you ok?' Hardik Pandya's girlfriend Mahieka snaps at photographers
content: Hardik Pandya's girlfriend, Mahieka Sharma, protected cricketer's son Agastya from intrusive photographers after a Mumbai Indians match.  She shielded the child from camera flashes, visibly annoyed by the crowd's behavior. Meanwhile, Mumbai Indians secured a thrilling six-wicket victory against Kolkata Knight Riders, marking their first season-opening win since 2012.

content: fantasy baseball buzz file aims to provide fantasy managers with the intel they need as news breaks around baseball . three players making their MLB debuts homered on an opening day for the first time in history . two rookies hit home runs in each of their first three big-league games .

content: the 2026 men's final four is set for saturday's national semifinals against Illinois . the final weekend of the tournament is the final of the NCAA tournament . for the season, Arizona ranked fifth in the country in paint points, averaging 42 .

title: Burqa-clad Hindu woman stopped at Varanasi airport on way to marry Muslim man, court lets her go
content: a 22-year-old woman was detained along with a youth at the lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport in Varanasi . the woman from Gopalganj in Bihar had to board a Mumbai flight with the youth . airline officials grew suspicious when they saw her in burqa . after initial questioning, CISF handed her and the youth accompanying her to police .
